generatedSentence: Leo, a picker for international independents, often found his vision messy and unbalanced after long shifts repacking items. He was swiftly employed, but his boss, a big man with a gargoyle-like pitchfork collection, often seemed bonkers. Leo, though frequently entertained by the strange setup, sometimes felt his tailbone sicken, knowing he could easily be flaked or mislead if things weren't redone to his picky standards. He secretly hyped a vanishing monument, a solo dream, unwatched. One night, a sudden power outage made his apartment go dark. Tired, he ate stale donuts and considered his impractical dreams of solo skydive hosting, a vivid fantasy born from watching deejays. His flatmates, always up for an affray, suggested brandies. He knew he needed to retry, ensure adherence to his goals, but the thought of a destroyer, a vampire-like entity, seemed to pause his muscle, making his efforts jammy and difficult, like trying to skyjack a plane with hamsters. He decided to keep a plant, a fantastic cucumber, to inject some life into his room. A comma in his dreams, a transparent tentacle appeared, unimposing yet powerful, offering answers. He felt a kamikaze urge, a need to abridge his fears and achieve his peak potential. This funny vision, perhaps fueled by nicotine and ibuprofen, gave him the ballast he needed to logoff from his old life, to become an independent thinker, a true wizard. With renewed resolve, he worked to locate the hidden armoury of his mind. He found a daisy, a nose-fluff of confidence, and a glimpse of a satyr with a cabriolet. His mistress, a shadowy figure he'd only seen in dreams, gave him a macadamia nut. He felt Hallelujah! He was no longer a dummy, no longer a vagrant in his own mind. He knew he could mend his rating, iron out his past, and transcend his own obituary.
panelSentences: Leo, a picker for international independents, often found his vision messy and unbalanced after long shifts repacking items. He was swiftly employed, but his boss, a big man with a gargoyle-like pitchfork collection, often seemed bonkers. Leo, though frequently entertained by the strange setup, sometimes felt his tailbone sicken, knowing he could easily be flaked or mislead if things weren't redone to his picky standards. He secretly hyped a vanishing monument, a solo dream, unwatched.

One night, a sudden power outage made his apartment go dark. Tired, he ate stale donuts and considered his impractical dreams of solo skydive hosting, a vivid fantasy born from watching deejays. His flatmates, always up for an affray, suggested brandies. He knew he needed to retry, ensure adherence to his goals, but the thought of a destroyer, a vampire-like entity, seemed to pause his muscle, making his efforts jammy and difficult, like trying to skyjack a plane with hamsters.

He decided to keep a plant, a fantastic cucumber, to inject some life into his room. A comma in his dreams, a transparent tentacle appeared, unimposing yet powerful, offering answers. He felt a kamikaze urge, a need to abridge his fears and achieve his peak potential. This funny vision, perhaps fueled by nicotine and ibuprofen, gave him the ballast he needed to logoff from his old life, to become an independent thinker, a true wizard.

With renewed resolve, he worked to locate the hidden armoury of his mind. He found a daisy, a nose-fluff of confidence, and a glimpse of a satyr with a cabriolet. His mistress, a shadowy figure he'd only seen in dreams, gave him a macadamia nut. He felt Hallelujah! He was no longer a dummy, no longer a vagrant in his own mind. He knew he could mend his rating, iron out his past, and transcend his own obituary.
rawPanelSentences: Leo, a picker for international independents, often found his vision messy and unbalanced after long shifts repacking items. He was swiftly employed, but his boss, a big man with a gargoyle-like pitchfork collection, often seemed bonkers. Leo, though frequently entertained by the strange setup, sometimes felt his tailbone sicken, knowing he could easily be flaked or mislead if things weren't redone to his picky standards. He secretly hyped a vanishing monument, a solo dream, unwatched.

One night, a sudden power outage made his apartment go dark. Tired, he ate stale donuts and considered his impractical dreams of solo skydive hosting, a vivid fantasy born from watching deejays. His flatmates, always up for an affray, suggested brandies. He knew he needed to retry, ensure adherence to his goals, but the thought of a destroyer, a vampire-like entity, seemed to pause his muscle, making his efforts jammy and difficult, like trying to skyjack a plane with hamsters.

He decided to keep a plant, a fantastic cucumber, to inject some life into his room. A comma in his dreams, a transparent tentacle appeared, unimposing yet powerful, offering answers. He felt a kamikaze urge, a need to abridge his fears and achieve his peak potential. This funny vision, perhaps fueled by nicotine and ibuprofen, gave him the ballast he needed to logoff from his old life, to become an independent thinker, a true wizard.

With renewed resolve, he worked to locate the hidden armoury of his mind. He found a daisy, a nose-fluff of confidence, and a glimpse of a satyr with a cabriolet. His mistress, a shadowy figure he'd only seen in dreams, gave him a macadamia nut. He felt Hallelujah! He was no longer a dummy, no longer a vagrant in his own mind. He knew he could mend his rating, iron out his past, and transcend his own obituary.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a wiry figure with glowing blue eyes, wearing a simple, dark jumpsuit with silver piping and a singular ear cuff. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A lone figure sits hunched over a workbench cluttered with glowing tech, surrounded by shelves of bizarre repacked items, a massive, shadowed gargoyle statue looming in the background, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The figure stands silhouetted against a futuristic city skyline during a power outage, a single bright star shining above, low-angle shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A transparent, glowing tentacle extends from a potted plant towards the figure's hand, who looks on with a surprised but determined expression, medium close-up. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The figure, now standing tall and confident, gazes towards a distant, shimmering portal, a subtle daisy emblem on their chest, full body shot.

definitions.definition: To introduce (something) suddenly or violently.
definitions.example: Punk injected a much-needed sense of urgency into the British music scene.

definitions.definition: To administer an injection to (someone or something), especially of medicine or drugs.
definitions.example: Now lie back while we inject you with the anesthetic.

definitions.definition: To take or be administered something by means of injection, especially medicine or drugs.
definitions.example: It's been a week since I stopped injecting, and I'm still in withdrawal.

definitions.definition: To introduce (code) into an existing program or its memory space, often without tight integration and sometimes through a security vulnerability.
To cast or throw; used with on.
The action of refreshing; a means of restoring strength, energy or vigour.
A light snack or drink.
The punctuation mark ⟨,⟩ used to indicate a set off parts of a sentence or between elements of a list.
A similar-looking subscript diacritical mark.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the genus Polygonia, having a comma-shaped white mark on the underwings, especially Polygonia c-album and Polygonia c-aureum of North Africa, Europe, and Asia.
A difference in the calculation of nearly identical intervals by different ways.
A delimiting marker between items in a genetic sequence.

In Ancient Greek rhetoric, a short clause, something less than a colon, originally denoted by comma marks. In antiquity it was defined as a combination of words having no more than eight syllables in all. It was later applied to longer phrases, e.g. the Johannine comma.
A brief interval.
To place a comma or commas within text; to follow, precede, or surround a portion of text with commas.
A large construction used in warfare, such as a battering ram, catapult etc.
A tool; a utensil or implement.
A complex mechanical device which converts energy into useful motion or physical effects.
A person or group of people which influence a larger group; a driving force.
The part of a car or other vehicle which provides the force for motion, now especially one powered by internal combustion.
A self-powered vehicle, especially a locomotive, used for pulling cars along a track.
A software or hardware system responsible for a specific technical task (usually with qualifying word).
definitions.example: a graphics engine

definitions.definition: Ingenuity; cunning, trickery, guile.
The result of cunning; something ingenious, a contrivance; (in negative senses) a plot, a scheme.
Natural talent; genius.
Anything used to effect a purpose; any device or contrivance; an agent.
To equip with an engine; said especially of steam vessels.
definitions.example: Vessels are often built by one firm and engined by another.

definitions.definition: To assault with an engine.
To contrive; to put into action.
To rack; to torture.
A mythological undead creature said to feed on the blood of the living.
A person with the medical condition systemic lupus erythematosus, colloquially known as vampirism, with effects such as photosensitivity and brownish-red stained teeth.
A blood-sucking bat; vampire bat (Desmodus rotundus)
A person who drains one's time, energy, money, etc.
A vamp: a seductive woman who exploits men.
A medical technician who works with patients' blood.
To drain of energy or resources.
(of a material or object) See-through, clear; having the property that light passes through it almost undisturbed, such that one can see through it clearly.
definitions.example: The waters of the lake were transparent until the factory dumped waste there.

definitions.definition: (of a system or organization) Open, public; having the property that theories and practices are publicly visible, thereby reducing the chance of corruption.
Obvious; readily apparent; easy to see or understand.
definitions.example: His reasons for the decision were transparent.

definitions.definition: Having the property of transparency, i.e. sufficiently accurate that the compressed result is perceptually indistinguishable from the uncompressed input.
Not noticeable because it happens automatically or in the background; invisible.
An attack requiring the suicide of the one carrying it out, especially when done with an aircraft.
One who carries out a suicide attack, especially with an aircraft.
One who takes excessive risks, as for example in a sporting event.
A cocktail made of equal parts vodka, triple sec and lime juice.
A deliberate wipeout.
To destroy (a ship, etc.) in a suicide attack, especially by crashing an aircraft.
To carry out a suicide attack, especially by crashing an aircraft.
To fail disastrously.
Suicidal, risking one's own life
A go-cart.
A point; the sharp end or top of anything that terminates in a point; as, the peak, or front, of a cap.
The highest value reached by some quantity in a time period.
definitions.example: The stock market reached a peak in September 1929.

definitions.definition: The top, or one of the tops, of a hill, mountain, or range, ending in a point.
definitions.example: They reached the peak after 8 hours of climbing.

definitions.definition: The whole hill or mountain, especially when isolated.
The upper aftermost corner of a fore-and-aft sail.
definitions.example: peak-brails

definitions.definition: The narrow part of a vessel's bow, or the hold within it.
The extremity of an anchor fluke; the bill.
A local maximum of a function, e.g. for sine waves, each point at which the value of y is at its maximum.
To reach a highest degree or maximum.
definitions.example: Historians argue about when the Roman Empire began to peak and ultimately decay.

definitions.definition: To rise or extend into a peak or point; to form, or appear as, a peak.
To raise the point of (a gaff) closer to perpendicular.
Maximal, maximally quintessential or representative; constituting the culmination of
Bad
Unlucky; unfortunate
A silent person; a person who does not talk.
An unintelligent person.
definitions.example: Don't be such a dummy!

definitions.definition: A figure of a person or animal used by a ventriloquist; a puppet.
Something constructed with the size and form of a human, to be used in place of a person.
definitions.example: To understand the effects of the accident, we dropped a dummy from the rooftop.

definitions.definition: A person who is the mere tool of another; a man of straw.
A deliberately nonfunctional device or tool used in place of a functional one.
definitions.example: The hammer and drill in the display are dummies.

definitions.definition: A "dummy teat"; a plastic or rubber teat used to soothe or comfort a baby; a pacifier.
definitions.example: The baby wants her dummy.

definitions.definition: A player whose hand is shown and is to be played from by another player.
A word serving only to make a construction grammatical.
definitions.example: The pronoun "it" in "It's a mystery why this happened" is a dummy.

definitions.definition: An unused parameter or value.
definitions.example: If flag1 is false, the other parameters are dummies.

definitions.definition: A feigned pass or kick or play in order to deceive an opponent.
A bodily gesture meant to fool an opposing player; a feint.
To make a mock-up or prototype version of something, without some or all off its intended functionality.
definitions.example: The carpenters dummied some props for the rehearsals.

definitions.definition: To feint.
A person with whom one shares a flat.
A person with whom one shares any rental dwelling, not necessarily a flat.
An automobile with a retractable top.
A light two- or four-wheeled carriage with a folding top, pulled by a single horse.
To hold spellbound; to bewitch, charm or captivate.
To make subservient; to enslave or subjugate.
Not imposing; not grand or magnificent; modest.
An elongated, boneless, flexible organ or limb of some animals, such as the octopus and squid.
One of the glandular hairs on the leaves of certain insectivorous plants.
An insidious reach or influence.
definitions.example: the tentacles of the criminal underworld

definitions.definition: Regardless; anyhow.
definitions.example: He didn't enjoy washing his car, but it was so dirty that he did it anyway.

definitions.definition: Used to indicate that a statement explains or supports a previous statement. See anyhow and at least.
definitions.example: I don't think that's true. I haven't found any evidence, anyway.

definitions.definition: Used to indicate a change of subject.
Used at the end of a question for emphasis, or to direct the conversation to something of more broad importance (compare with more to the point).
In any way.
To deprive; to cut off.
To debar from.
To make shorter; to shorten in duration or extent.
To shorten or contract by using fewer words, yet retaining the sense; to epitomize; to condense.
Cut short; truncate.
To curtail.
definitions.example: He had his rights abridged by the crooked sheriff.

definitions.definition: A joke.
A comic strip.
Amusing; humorous; comical.
definitions.example: When I went to the circus, I only found the clowns funny.

definitions.definition: Strange or unusual, often implying unpleasant.
definitions.example: I've got a funny feeling that this isn't going to work.

definitions.definition: Showing unexpected resentment.
In an unusual manner; strangely.
An NSAID, isobutylphenyl propionic acid.
A hindrance; that which impedes or obstructs progress.
A disability, especially one affecting the hearing or speech.
definitions.example: Working in a noisy factory left me with a slight hearing impediment.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the plural) Baggage, especially that of an army; impedimenta.
A vine in the gourd family, Cucumis sativus.
The edible fruit of this plant, having a green rind and crisp white flesh.
An adult male human.
definitions.example: The show is especially popular with middle-aged men.

definitions.definition: (collective) All human males collectively: mankind.
A human, a person of either gender, usually an adult. (See usage notes.)
definitions.example: every man for himself

definitions.definition: (collective) All humans collectively: mankind, humankind, humanity. (Sometimes capitalized as Man.)
A member of the genus Homo, especially of the species Homo sapiens.
An male person, usually an adult; a (generally adult male) sentient being, whether human, supernatural, elf, alien, etc.
An adult male who has, to an eminent degree, qualities considered masculine, such as strength, integrity, and devotion to family; a mensch.
Manliness; the quality or state of being manly.
A husband.
A lover; a boyfriend.
A male enthusiast or devotee; a male who is very fond of or devoted to a specified kind of thing. (Used as the last element of a compound.)
definitions.example: Some people prefer apple pie, but me, I’m a cherry pie man.

definitions.definition: A person, usually male, who has duties or skills associated with a specified thing. (Used as the last element of a compound.)
definitions.example: I wanted to be a guitar man on a road tour, but instead I’m a flag man on a road crew.

definitions.definition: A person, usually male, who can fulfill one's requirements with regard to a specified matter.
A male who belongs to a particular group: an employee, a student or alumnus, a representative, etc.
An adult male servant.
A vassal; a subject.
definitions.example: Like master, like man.

definitions.definition: A piece or token used in board games such as chess.
Used to refer to oneself or one's group: I, we; construed in the third person.
A term of familiar address often implying on the part of the speaker some degree of authority, impatience, or haste.
definitions.example: Come on, man, we've got no time to lose!

definitions.definition: A friendly term of address usually reserved for other adult males.
definitions.example: Hey, man, how's it goin'?

definitions.definition: A player on whom another is playing, with the intent of limiting their attacking impact.
To supply (something) with staff or crew (of either sex).
definitions.example: The ship was manned with a small crew.

definitions.definition: To take up position in order to operate (something).
definitions.example: Man the machine guns!

definitions.definition: (possibly obsolete) To brace (oneself), to fortify or steel (oneself) in a manly way. (Compare man up.)
To wait on, attend to or escort.
To accustom (a raptor or other type of bird) to the presence of people.
A brief notice of a person’s death, as published in a newspaper.
A biography of a recently deceased person, written by a journalist and published in a newspaper.
A register of deaths in a monastery.
Relating to the death of a person.
A person who wanders from place to place; a nomad, a wanderer.
(specifically) A person without settled employment or habitation who supports himself or herself by begging or some dishonest means; a tramp, a vagabond.
definitions.example: Every morning before work, I see that poor vagrant around the neighbourhood begging for food.

definitions.definition: Vagrans egista, a widely distributed Asian butterfly of the family Nymphalidae.
An animal, typically a bird, found outside its species' usual range.
A response or reply; something said or done in reaction to a statement or question.
definitions.example: Her answer to his proposal was a slap in the face.

definitions.definition: A solution to a problem.
definitions.example: There is no simple answer to corruption.

definitions.definition: A document filed in response to a complaint, responding to each point raised in the complaint and raising counterpoints.
To make a reply or response to.
definitions.example: He answered the question.

definitions.definition: To speak in defence against; to reply to in defence.
definitions.example: to answer a charge or accusation

definitions.definition: To respond to a call by someone at a door or telephone, or other similar piece of equipment.
definitions.example: Nobody answered when I knocked on the door.

definitions.definition: To suit a need or purpose satisfactorily.
definitions.example: It answers the need.

definitions.definition: To be accountable or responsible; to make amends.
definitions.example: He has a lot to answer for.

definitions.definition: To file a document in response to a complaint.
To correspond to; to be in harmony with; to be in agreement with.
To be opposite, or to act in opposition.
To be or act in conformity, or by way of accommodation, correspondence, relation, or proportion; to conform; to correspond; to suit; usually with to.
To respond to satisfactorily; to meet successfully by way of explanation, argument, or justification; to refute.
To be or act in compliance with, in fulfillment or satisfaction of, as an order, obligation, or demand.
definitions.example: He answered my claim upon him.

definitions.definition: To render account to or for.
To atone for; to be punished for.
To be or act as an equivalent to, or as adequate or sufficient for; to serve for; to repay.
A wild flowering plant Bellis perennis of the Asteraceae family, with a yellow head and white petals
Many other flowering plants of various species.
A boot or other footwear.
Heavy material that is placed in the hold of a ship (or in the gondola of a balloon), to provide stability.
Anything that steadies emotion or the mind.
Coarse gravel or similar material laid to form a bed for roads or railroads, or in making concrete.
A material, such as aggregate or precast concrete pavers, which employs its mass and the force of gravity to hold single-ply roof membranes in place.
Device used for stabilizing current in an electric circuit (e.g. in a tube lamp supply circuit)
That which gives, or helps to maintain, uprightness, steadiness, and security.
To stabilize or load a ship with ballast.
To lay ballast on the bed of a railroad track.
A protuberance on the face housing the nostrils, which are used to breathe or smell.
definitions.example: She has a cold in the nose.

definitions.definition: A snout, the nose of an animal.
The tip of an object.
definitions.example: the nose of a tea-kettle, a bellows, or a fighter plane

definitions.definition: The bulge on the side of a piece of a jigsaw puzzle, that fits into the hole of its adjacent piece.
The length of a horse’s nose, used to indicate the distance between horses at the finish of a race, or any very close race.
definitions.example: Red Rum only won by a nose.

definitions.definition: A perfumer.
The power of smelling.
Bouquet, the smell of something, especially wine.
The skill in recognising bouquet.
definitions.example: It is essential that a winetaster develops a good nose.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Skill at finding information.
definitions.example: A successful reporter has a nose for news.

definitions.definition: A downward projection from a cornice.
An informer.
To move cautiously by advancing its front end.
definitions.example: The ship nosed through the minefield.

definitions.definition: To snoop.
definitions.example: She was nosing around other people’s business.

definitions.definition: To detect by smell or as if by smell.
To push with one's nose; to nuzzle.
To defeat (as in a race or other contest) by a narrow margin; sometimes with out.
To utter in a nasal manner; to pronounce with a nasal twang.
definitions.example: to nose a prayer

definitions.definition: To furnish with a nose.
definitions.example: to nose a stair tread

definitions.definition: To confront; be closely face to face or opposite to.
Anything light, soft or fuzzy, especially fur, hair, feathers.
Anything inconsequential or superficial.
definitions.example: That article was basically a bunch of fluff. It didn't say anything substantive.

definitions.definition: A lapse or mistake, especially a mistake in an actor's lines.
Marshmallow creme.
definitions.example: That New England-style salami and fluff sandwich sure hit the spot!

definitions.definition: A passive partner in a lesbian relationship.
A fart.
To make something fluffy.
definitions.example: The cat fluffed its tail.

definitions.definition: To become fluffy, puff up.
To move lightly like fluff.
(of an actor or announcer) To make a mistake in one's lines.
To do incorrectly, for example mishit, miskick, miscue etc.
To fart.
To arouse (a male pornographic actor) before filming.
A candidate or voter not affiliated with any political party, a freethinker, free of a party platform.
A neutral or uncommitted person.
A team not affiliated with any league or conference.
Not dependent; not contingent or depending on something else; free.
Not affiliated with any political party.
definitions.example: the independent candidate

definitions.definition: Providing a comfortable livelihood.
definitions.example: an independent property

definitions.definition: Not subject to bias or influence; self-directing.
definitions.example: a man of an independent mind

definitions.definition: Separate from; exclusive; irrespective.
To assign or be assigned a particular rank or level.
definitions.example: She is rated fourth in the country.

definitions.definition: To evaluate or estimate the value of.
definitions.example: They rate his talents highly.

definitions.definition: To consider or regard.
definitions.example: He rated this book brilliant.

definitions.definition: To deserve; to be worth.
definitions.example: The view here hardly rates a mention in the travel guide.

definitions.definition: To determine the limits of safe functioning for a machine or electrical device.
definitions.example: The transformer is rated at 10 watts.

definitions.definition: To evaluate a property's value for the purposes of local taxation.
To like; to think highly of.
definitions.example: The customers don't rate the new burgers.

definitions.definition: To have position (in a certain class).
definitions.example: He rates as the best cyclist in the country.

definitions.definition: To have value or standing.
definitions.example: This last performance of hers didn't rate very high with the judges.

definitions.definition: To ratify.
To ascertain the exact rate of the gain or loss of (a chronometer) as compared with true time.
To berate, scold.
A position on a scale
An evaluation of status, especially of financial status
definitions.example: They have a poor credit rating.

definitions.definition: A number, letter, or other mark that refers to the ability of something.
definitions.example: He has a high chess rating.

definitions.definition: A seaman in a warship
The status of a seaman, corresponding to rank in officers.
A woodland creature with pointed ears, legs, and short horns of a goat and a fondness for unrestrained revelry.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the genus Faunis.

A sylvan deity or demigod, male companion of Pan or Dionysus, represented as part man and part goat, and characterized by riotous merriment and lasciviousness, sometimes pictured with a perpetual erection.
(by extension) A lecherous man.
Any of various butterflies of the nymphalid subfamily Satyrinae, having brown wings marked with eyelike spots; a meadow brown.
The orangutan.
To place; to set in a particular spot or position.
To find out where something is located.
To designate the site or place of; to define the limits of (Note: the designation may be purely descriptive: it need not be prescriptive.)
definitions.example: The council must locate the new hospital

definitions.definition: To place oneself; to take up one's residence; to settle.
A woman, specifically one with great control, authority or ownership
definitions.example: (male equivalent) master

definitions.definition: A female teacher
definitions.example: (male equivalent) master

definitions.definition: The other woman in an extramarital relationship, generally including sexual relations
A dominatrix
definitions.example: (male equivalent) master

definitions.definition: A woman well skilled in anything, or having the mastery over it
A woman regarded with love and devotion; a sweetheart
A married woman; a wife
The jack in the game of bowls
A female companion to a master (a man with control, authority or ownership)
Of a woman: to master; to learn or develop to a high degree of proficiency.
To act or take the role of a mistress.
An evergreen tree, of the genus Macadamia, native to Australia and cultivated in Hawaii.
The fruit of this tree; the macadamia nut.
A viscous orange-brown liquid obtained by chemical treatment of cellulose and used as the basis of manufacturing rayon and cellulose film.
A fabric made from this material.
A semicircular Aboriginal dwelling, intended as a temporary shelter from the heat of the sun.
Someone, usually male, who uses (or has skill with) magic, mystic items, and magical and mystical practices.
One who is especially skilled or unusually talented in a particular field.
definitions.example: He was a financial wizard, capable of predicting the movements of the stock markets.

definitions.definition: A computer program or script used to simplify complex operations, often for an inexperienced user.
definitions.example: Use the "Add Network Connection" wizard to connect to a network in a series of simple steps.

definitions.definition: One of the administrators of a multi-user dungeon.
A wise man; a sage.
An adult virgin over the age of 30.
To practice wizardry.
To conjure.
Fine, superb (originally RAF slang).
One who spends time thinking, contemplating or meditating.
An intellectual, such as a philosopher or theologian.
A shout of “Hallelujah”.
A song of praise to God; a musical composition based on the word "Hallelujah".
definitions.example: the hallelujah (chorus) in Handel's "Messiah"

definitions.definition: (in the plural) General praise.
To cry "hallelujah" in praise.
An exclamation used in songs of praise or thanksgiving to God.
A general expression of gratitude or adoration.
definitions.example: Hallelujah! It’s finally the weekend!

definitions.definition: Located or positioned out of sight; not visually apparent.
definitions.example: hidden treasure; hidden talents

definitions.definition: Obscure.
To put (something) in a place where it will be harder to discover or out of sight.
definitions.example: He hides his magazines under the bed.

definitions.definition: To put oneself in a place where one will be harder to find or out of sight.
Glasses, jugs and other tableware made of glass.
A brief look, glance, or peek.
definitions.example: I only got a glimpse of the car, so I can tell you the colour but not the registration number.

definitions.definition: A sudden flash.
A faint idea; an inkling.
To see or view briefly or incompletely.
definitions.example: I have only begun to glimpse the magnitude of the problem.

definitions.definition: To appear by glimpses.
To pass an iron over (clothing or some other item made of cloth) in order to remove creases.
To shackle with irons; to fetter or handcuff.
To furnish or arm with iron.
definitions.example: to iron a wagon

definitions.definition: The act of pressing clothes with an iron.
definitions.example: I hate ironing.

definitions.definition: Laundry that has been washed and is ready to be ironed.
definitions.example: I really should make a start on the ironing soon.

definitions.definition: Laundry that has recently been ironed.
definitions.example: I really should put the ironing away.
